commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5c366b67ea73afeb8301aa67b46580c1b0a438bd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Z.AI OpenAI-completions thinking payloads to use `thinking: { type }`, forward provider effort values, share that mapping across both request builders, and add focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +48, Tests +213. Total +261 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main still sends the stale `enable_thinking` field for Z.AI in one builder and lacks Z.AI `thinking` handling in the managed transport builder, matching the linked report and official API contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for the type-check defect and external live-provider proof; do not queue automated repair while the external PR proof gate is unmet.

target.title: fix(zai): send current GLM thinking params
target.body:
Closes #97772

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users enable reasoning on Z.ai GLM models but OpenClaw sends the stale `enable_thinking` payload field, so current GLM reasoning mode is not activated for GLM-5.2 and no `reasoning_content` is returned.

## Why This Change Was Made

The OpenAI-compatible completions formatter now emits the current Z.ai thinking payload shape, `thinking: { type: "enabled" | "disabled" }`, instead of the legacy `enable_thinking` field. The shared Z.ai mapping is used by both OpenAI-compatible completions request builders, so the normal stream path and the managed provider-transport path stay aligned.

GLM-5.2 receives the provider-supported `reasoning_effort` values (`high` or `max`) when thinking is enabled. `minimal`, `none`, and off-style requests send disabled thinking without `reasoning_effort`, and binary Z.ai thinking models such as GLM-5.1 keep the on/off payload without an unsupported effort field.

## User Impact

Z.ai GLM-5.2 users can turn reasoning on and have OpenClaw send the request shape expected by the current Z.ai API. Existing binary thinking models keep their current behavior and do not receive extra unsupported `reasoning_effort` parameters.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: both OpenAI-compatible completions request builders use `thinking.type`, never `enable_thinking`, for Z.ai thinking mode; GLM-5.2 maps enabled low-style levels to `reasoning_effort: "high"`, strongest OpenClaw effort to `"max"`, treats `minimal` as disabled thinking, and does not add `reasoning_effort` for binary GLM-5.1.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` -> 2 Vitest shards passed; 39 + 285 tests passed.
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-completions-zai-thinking.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts packages/llm-core/src/types.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `git diff --check` -> no whitespace errors.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings reported.
- Observed result: focused payload tests intercept the legacy `streamOpenAICompletions` request body before network and directly inspect `buildOpenAICompletionsParams` for the managed transport path, verifying the current Z.ai thinking fields and effort gating in both builders.
- ClawSweeper follow-up handled: added managed OpenAI-completions transport coverage and changed `minimal` to disabled thinking with no `reasoning_effort`.
- Not tested / proof gaps: no live Z.ai API call was run from this environment. I checked the local environment for common Z.ai key names (`ZAI_API_KEY`, `Z_AI_API_KEY`, `OPENCLAW_ZAI_API_KEY`, `GLM_API_KEY`, `ZHIPUAI_API_KEY`) and none were present, so this PR still does not include a real GLM-5.2 response containing `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. Codex inspected the issue, checked duplicate PR coverage, implemented the focused fix, ran validation, addressed autoreview findings, and updated the PR after ClawSweeper feedback.
target.author: qingminglong

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
